As an authorized Texa dealer, we carry the full lineup of professional diagnostic tools — from the flagship Axone Voice with its revolutionary voice command technology, to the Konfort series of AC service stations, to dedicated EV diagnostic platforms. Every product we sell comes with our commitment to local support, expert guidance, and fast shipping to all 50 states.

Whether you run a single-bay independent shop or manage a fleet of hundreds, AutoTek has the right diagnostic solution for your operation. Give us a call at 314-922-3083 to discuss your needs, schedule a product demo, or get a personalized quote. We're here to help you work smarter, diagnose faster, and grow your business.

title: Texa Coverage: Why More Makes and Models Matters
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Vehicle coverage is the single most important factor when choosing a diagnostic tool. Here's why Texa's industry-leading coverage gives your shop a competitive edge.
content:
When a customer drives into your bay, you need to diagnose their vehicle — regardless of what they're driving. That's why vehicle coverage is the number one consideration when investing in diagnostic equipment. A tool that can't read the codes on a European sedan or a Japanese hybrid is a tool that's costing you money every time you have to turn a customer away.

Texa has built its reputation on comprehensive, multi-brand coverage that spans passenger cars, light trucks, heavy-duty commercial vehicles, motorcycles, marine engines, and off-highway equipment. Their development team works directly with vehicle manufacturers to ensure coverage is available as close to the vehicle's release date as possible. That means you're not waiting months or years for your diagnostic tool to catch up with what's rolling into your shop.

With real-time cloud updates delivered wirelessly to the Axone platform, your coverage database is always current. New models, new systems, new protocols — they're all pushed to your device automatically. This isn't just about having more logos on a compatibility chart. It's about never having to say 'sorry, we can't help you' to a paying customer.

title: How to Choose the Right Diagnostic Package for Your Fleet
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Fleet diagnostics is a different game than single-vehicle repair. Here's how to evaluate diagnostic packages and find the right fit for your fleet size and vehicle mix.
content:
Managing a fleet means managing complexity. You're dealing with multiple vehicle makes, models, and model years — often across different vehicle classes from light-duty vans to Class 8 trucks. Your diagnostic solution needs to handle all of it without requiring your technicians to juggle multiple tools and subscriptions.

The first step in choosing the right package is understanding your fleet composition. What percentage of your vehicles are light-duty versus heavy-duty? Do you have any electric or hybrid units? Are you running specialized equipment like refrigeration units or hydraulic systems? The answers to these questions will determine which diagnostic capabilities you need and which you can skip.

Texa's Deluxe Fleet Package is designed specifically for this challenge. It bundles the diagnostic hardware, comprehensive vehicle coverage, and fleet management software into a single solution. The centralized dashboard lets you track repair histories, schedule preventive maintenance, and generate cost reports across your entire fleet. For fleet managers who need to minimize downtime and control maintenance costs, it's a game-changer. Call us at 314-922-3083 to discuss your fleet's specific needs.

title: Understanding High-Voltage EV Safety in the Shop
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Working on electric vehicles requires a fundamentally different approach to safety. Here's what every shop owner and technician needs to know about high-voltage EV systems.
content:
Electric vehicles are no longer a novelty — they're a growing segment of the vehicles coming into your shop. But working on EVs requires a fundamentally different approach to safety compared to traditional internal combustion vehicles. High-voltage battery systems in modern EVs operate at 400 to 800 volts, and mishandling these systems can result in serious injury or death.

The first rule of EV safety is proper training. Every technician who works on electric or hybrid vehicles should be trained in high-voltage safety procedures, including how to properly de-energize a vehicle, how to test for residual voltage, and how to use insulated tools and PPE. Many states are beginning to require specific EV certifications, and insurance companies are paying close attention to shop safety protocols.

Texa's E Truck diagnostic platform is built with safety as a core design principle. The tool includes guided de-energization procedures, insulation resistance testing, and real-time monitoring of high-voltage system status throughout the diagnostic process. It takes the guesswork out of EV diagnostics and gives your technicians confidence that they're working safely. If your shop isn't prepared for EVs yet, now is the time to invest.

title: 5 Signs Your Diagnostic Tool is Holding You Back
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Your diagnostic tool should make you faster and more accurate. If you're experiencing these five problems, it might be time for an upgrade.
content:
A diagnostic tool is only valuable if it's actually helping you diagnose vehicles efficiently. Too many shops are limping along with outdated or underpowered tools that cost them time, money, and customers. Here are five signs that your current diagnostic tool is holding your shop back.

First, if you're regularly encountering vehicles that your tool can't communicate with, that's a coverage problem. Second, if your tool takes more than a few seconds to connect and pull codes, it's slowing down your workflow. Third, if you don't have access to bidirectional controls, guided diagnostics, or wiring diagrams, you're doing more guesswork than necessary. Fourth, if your software updates require a trip to a dealer or a complicated download process, you're falling behind on new vehicle coverage. And fifth, if your tool can't generate professional reports for your customers, you're missing an opportunity to build trust and justify your repair recommendations.

Modern diagnostic platforms like the Texa Axone Voice address all of these pain points. With comprehensive multi-brand coverage, lightning-fast connectivity, voice-activated operation, wireless cloud updates, and built-in reporting, it's designed to make your technicians more productive from day one. If any of those five signs sound familiar, give us a call at 314-922-3083 and let's talk about an upgrade.

title: Fleet Diagnostics: Keeping Your Business Moving
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Downtime is the enemy of every fleet operation. Here's how the right diagnostic strategy can keep your vehicles on the road and your business profitable.
content:
In fleet operations, every minute a vehicle sits in the shop is a minute it's not generating revenue. Whether you're running a delivery service, a construction company, or a long-haul trucking operation, downtime hits your bottom line hard. That's why a proactive diagnostic strategy isn't just a nice-to-have — it's a business necessity.

The key to minimizing downtime is shifting from reactive to preventive maintenance. Instead of waiting for a breakdown and then scrambling to diagnose and repair, smart fleet operators use diagnostic tools to identify developing issues before they become roadside emergencies. Regular diagnostic scans can catch things like degrading sensors, intermittent communication faults, and early signs of component failure — all before the driver notices a problem.

Texa's Deluxe Fleet Package makes this approach practical and scalable. The fleet management software lets you schedule regular diagnostic check-ins, track trends across your vehicle population, and prioritize repairs based on severity and urgency. The result is fewer unexpected breakdowns, lower repair costs, and more vehicles on the road generating revenue. That's the kind of ROI that pays for the diagnostic investment many times over.

title: AC Service Done Right: The Konfort Advantage
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: AC service is one of the most profitable services a shop can offer. Here's how Texa's Konfort series helps you do it faster, cleaner, and more accurately.
content:
Automotive AC service is a bread-and-butter revenue stream for shops across the country. But the days of simple R134a recovery and recharge are evolving. With the transition to R1234yf refrigerant, stricter EPA regulations, and increasingly complex HVAC systems in modern vehicles, shops need equipment that can keep up.

Texa's Konfort series of AC service stations represents the current state of the art in automotive climate service. These fully automatic machines handle the entire service cycle — recovery, recycling, vacuum, leak testing, and precision recharging — with minimal technician intervention. The built-in refrigerant identifier prevents the cross-contamination that can destroy a compressor and cost thousands in warranty claims. And the precision electronic scale ensures that every charge is accurate to the gram.

What really sets the Konfort apart is the large touchscreen interface with guided service procedures. Even a less experienced technician can perform a professional AC service by following the on-screen prompts. That means more consistent results, fewer comebacks, and higher customer satisfaction. If your shop is still using older AC equipment, the Konfort series is an upgrade that pays for itself quickly through increased efficiency and reduced errors.

title: The Axone Voice: Hands-Free Diagnostics Are Here
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Imagine diagnosing a vehicle without taking your hands off the engine. Texa's Axone Voice makes it possible with AI-powered voice command technology.
content:
Every technician knows the frustration of trying to operate a diagnostic tool while their hands are deep in an engine bay or underneath a dashboard. You're holding a flashlight in one hand, a multimeter probe in the other, and you need to scroll through diagnostic menus on a tablet sitting three feet away. It's inefficient, it's frustrating, and it slows down your workflow.

Texa's Axone Voice solves this problem with AI-powered voice command technology. Simply tell the device what you want to do — read codes, view live data, run an actuator test — and it responds. Your hands stay on the vehicle, your eyes stay on the component, and the diagnostic tool works for you instead of the other way around. It's a genuinely transformative experience for technicians who've been tapping and scrolling on tablets for years.

Beyond voice control, the Axone Voice is a powerhouse diagnostic platform in its own right. The 10-inch HD touchscreen displays crisp wiring diagrams and live data graphs. Wireless Bluetooth connectivity eliminates cable clutter. Real-time cloud updates ensure you always have coverage for the latest vehicles. And comprehensive multi-brand support means one tool covers everything that rolls through your door. If you want to see the future of diagnostics, call us at 314-922-3083 for a demo.

title: Electric Vehicle Diagnostics: What Every Shop Needs to Know
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: EV adoption is accelerating faster than most shops are preparing for. Here's what you need to know to get ready for the electric future.
content:
The electric vehicle revolution isn't coming — it's here. EV sales are climbing year over year, and industry analysts predict that EVs will represent a significant portion of all new vehicle sales within the next decade. For independent shops and fleet operators, that means the vehicles coming through your doors are changing, and your diagnostic capabilities need to change with them.

Electric vehicles present unique diagnostic challenges that traditional tools simply can't address. High-voltage battery packs, electric drive motors, inverters, onboard chargers, and thermal management systems all require specialized diagnostic access. Standard OBD2 scanners can read basic powertrain codes, but they can't perform the deep-system diagnostics that EV repair demands — things like individual cell voltage monitoring, insulation resistance testing, and motor efficiency analysis.

Texa's E Truck platform was purpose-built for this new reality. It provides comprehensive diagnostic access to electric and hybrid commercial vehicles, with built-in safety protocols that protect your technicians when working with high-voltage systems. Investing in EV diagnostic capability now positions your shop as a leader in your market — the shop that can handle anything, including the vehicles of the future. Don't wait until you have to turn away EV customers to make this investment.

content:

The diagnostic tools that shops relied on five or ten years ago are struggling to keep pace with the technology rolling through their bays today. That's where Texa comes in.

Founded in Italy with over 30 years of experience in automotive diagnostics, Texa has earned a reputation as the tool of choice for dealerships and professional shops across Europe and around the world. Their commitment to comprehensive vehicle coverage, continuous software development, and innovative hardware design has made them a global leader. And now, with an expanding presence in the United States, American shops have access to the same world-class diagnostic capability.

What sets Texa apart isn't just one feature — it's the whole ecosystem. The Axone hardware platform is powerful and ergonomic. The software coverage spans cars, trucks, marine, off-highway, and now electric vehicles. The cloud-based update system keeps your tool current without manual downloads or dealer visits. And the support network ensures that when you need help, you get it. At AutoTek, we're proud to be an authorized Texa dealer because we believe these tools represent the future of our industry.
